primary_sources.finding: Sigstore uses trusted time to determine whether a short-lived signing certificate was valid when signing occurred; Rekor v1 integratedTime is covered by the signed entry timestamp but its clock is not externally verifiable, while RFC3161 timestamps are signed and independently verifiable.
Bundles using expired short-lived certificates must include at least one signed entry timestamp or RFC3161 timestamp that proves signing occurred inside the certificate validity window.

finding: The prior DSSE promotion gate required transparency verification but did not separately classify whether the asserted signing time was cryptographically bound and inside the certificate validity window.
design_implication: Cryptographic promotion should consume an explicit signing-time decision. Bare Rekor integratedTime must fail closed; verified RFC3161, verified Rekor v1 SET-bound time, or trusted Rekor v2 TSA time may pass only when the time is within certificate validity.

verification.route: isolated Node test run using byte-identical pre-commit files
cases: verified RFC3161 time accepted inside validity
Rekor v2 time without trusted TSA rejected

uncertainty: The classifier relies on external verifiers for timestamp signatures, TSA trust, certificate chains, and SET verification.
falsification_route: Pass a bare integratedTime, untrusted TSA result, invalid timestamp, or time outside certificate validity and observe rejection; any acceptance falsifies the intended contract.
privacy_boundary: Public standards and public repository code only; no private chat, health, credential, or identifying material used.
